projectDescription: Pipedream is a hosted integration platform for developers that enables building event-driven automations and workflows by connecting 2,800+ APIs with pre-built components or custom Node.js, Python, Go, and Bash code.
keyFeatures: Workflows: Build event-driven automations with sequences of steps triggered by HTTP, timers, or app events
Event Sources: Pre-built and custom triggers that watch for new data from 2,800+ integrated apps
Actions: Pre-built code steps for common operations across Pipedream's 1,000+ API integrations
Custom Code: Run Node.js, Python, Go, or Bash code with any npm/pip/go packages
Pipedream Connect: Embed OAuth account connections in your own app with SDK and React components
MCP Server: Expose 2,800+ API tools to AI agents via the Model Context Protocol
Destinations: Asynchronously deliver events to S3, Snowflake, HTTP, Email, and SSE
Free tier: Generous free plan for individual developers with SOC 2 compliance
publicApiSurface: defineComponent() - define a Pipedream workflow component
defineApp() - define a Pipedream app with auth and base methods
createBackendClient() - create a server-side Pipedream Connect client
createFrontendClient() - create a browser-side Pipedream Connect client
client.createConnectToken() - generate a short-lived token for frontend auth
client.getAccountById() - retrieve a connected account
client.getAccounts() - list connected accounts for a user
client.deleteAccount() - delete a connected account
client.deployTrigger() - deploy a Pipedream trigger for a user
client.runAction() - run a Pipedream action step on behalf of a user
ComponentFormContainer - React component to embed a Pipedream component form
FrontendClientProvider - React context provider for the frontend SDK client
CustomizeProvider - React component for theming/styling ComponentForm
useAccounts() - React hook to list connected accounts
useApps() - React hook to list available Pipedream apps
useComponents() - React hook to list actions/triggers for an app
$send.s3() - send data to an Amazon S3 destination
$send.http() - send data to an HTTP destination
$send.email() - send data to an email destination
$send.sse() - send data to an SSE stream destination
$.export() - export a value from a workflow step
$.respond() - send an HTTP response from a workflow
